summary: Day-2 operations for the sample ERP S3 estate: lifecycle retention rules, versioning and public-access-block guardrails, tag hygiene, cross-region replication (engineer-authored), and bucket teardown (engineer-authored, irreversible).

operations.title: Add S3 lifecycle rule
operations.description: Appends a new lifecycle rule (transition to a cheaper storage class and/or expire objects) to the bucket's lifecycle configuration. Applies to matching objects going forward; AWS evaluates lifecycle rules within roughly 24-48 hours of the change, it does not act instantly.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket to add the rule to.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: Unique identifier for this rule within the bucket.

params.name: prefix
params.label: Object key prefix
params.help: Limit the rule to keys starting with this prefix; leave blank to apply to the whole bucket.

params.name: transition_days
params.label: Transition after (days)
params.help: Days after object creation before it transitions to the target storage class.

params.name: storage_class
params.label: Target storage class
params.help: Storage class objects move into after the transition period.

params.name: expiration_days
params.label: Expire after (days)
params.help: Days after which matching objects are permanently deleted; 0 means no expiration.

operations.title: Enable or suspend bucket versioning
operations.description: Switches object versioning on the bucket between Enabled and Suspended. Suspending stops new versions being created but keeps existing versions; once a bucket has been versioned it can never return to a fully unversioned state, so treat this as a one-way door on first enable.

params.name: versioning
params.label: Bucket versioning config
params.help: The bucket versioning resource to change.

params.name: status
params.label: New status
params.help: Enabled protects against accidental overwrite/delete; Suspended stops creating new versions.

operations.title: Update bucket tags
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the bucket. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on stored objects or access.

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Set public access block flags
operations.description: Adjusts the four S3 public-access-block controls on the bucket. Tightening them is a safe remediation for accidentally-public buckets; relaxing any of them can expose bucket contents or policy to the internet, so both directions require review.

params.name: pab
params.label: Public access block config
params.help: The bucket's public-access-block resource to change.

params.name: block_public_acls
params.label: Block public ACLs
params.help: Sets block_public_acls: when true, S3 rejects any new public ACL on the bucket or its objects; true is the safe hardening default, and relaxing it to false is the direction reviewers scrutinize.

params.name: block_public_policy
params.label: Block public bucket policies
params.help: Sets block_public_policy: when true, S3 rejects any bucket policy that would grant public access, so a mistyped policy cannot open the bucket to the internet; true is the safe default.

params.name: ignore_public_acls
params.label: Ignore public ACLs
params.help: Sets ignore_public_acls: when true, S3 treats every existing public ACL on the bucket and its objects as private, so anything made public in the past stops being publicly readable; true is the safe default.

params.name: restrict_public_buckets
params.label: Restrict public bucket policies
params.help: Sets restrict_public_buckets: when true, only the bucket owner's account and AWS services can use a public bucket policy, cutting off anonymous and cross-account access even if such a policy exists; true is the safe default.

operations.title: Enable cross-region replication
operations.description: Creates a new replication configuration to continuously copy objects from this bucket to a destination bucket (typically the DR region). Requires versioning already enabled on both source and destination and an existing IAM replication role; architectural change, must be authored and reviewed by an engineer.

params.name: bucket
params.label: Source S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket replication will be added to. Must already have versioning enabled.

params.name: destination_bucket_arn
params.label: Destination bucket ARN
params.help: ARN of the versioned destination bucket, e.g. in the DR region.

params.name: iam_role_arn
params.label: Replication IAM role ARN
params.help: Existing IAM role granting s3 replication permissions; this operation references it by ARN, it does not create the role.

params.name: storage_class
params.label: Destination storage class
params.help: Storage class replicated objects land in at the destination.

operations.title: Delete S3 bucket
operations.description: Permanently removes the bucket and its Terraform-managed configuration. Apply fails unless the bucket is already empty or force_destroy is set; all objects, versions and configuration are unrecoverable once deleted.

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket to permanently delete.

params.name: confirm_empty
params.label: Confirm bucket is empty or force-destroy acceptable
params.help: Must be explicitly confirmed; the deletion is unrecoverable.

operations.title: Set S3 Transfer Acceleration status
operations.description: Sets the legacy inline `acceleration_status` argument on the bucket to Enabled or Suspended, toggling S3 Transfer Acceleration. In-place and reversible; affects transfer routing and cost only, with no impact on stored objects or access. (Legacy inline field; the standalone aws_s3_bucket_accelerate_configuration is AWS's preferred equivalent but is not managed by this manifest.)

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket whose transfer-acceleration status to set.

params.name: acceleration_status
params.label: Acceleration status
params.help: Enabled turns on S3 Transfer Acceleration; Suspended turns it off. (Not usable in cn-north-1 or us-gov-west-1.)

operations.title: Set S3 bucket request payer
operations.description: Sets the legacy inline `request_payer` argument on the bucket to BucketOwner or Requester, controlling who bears the cost of requests and data transfer. Reversible in-place; switching to Requester can silently break downstream consumers that are not configured to pay, so the change is guardrailed. (Legacy inline field; the standalone aws_s3_bucket_request_payment_configuration is AWS's preferred equivalent but is not managed by this manifest.)

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket whose request-payer setting to change.

params.name: request_payer
params.label: Request payer
params.help: BucketOwner: the bucket owner pays for requests. Requester: the requester pays; consumers must send requester-pays headers or access breaks.

operations.title: Set legacy inline bucket canned ACL
operations.description: Sets the deprecated inline `acl` canned ACL on the bucket (e.g. private, public-read). This controls a public-access security boundary — public-read and public-read-write can expose bucket contents to the internet — so it is engineer-authored and reviewed before apply. In-place and reversible. (Legacy inline field; the standalone aws_s3_bucket_acl is AWS's preferred equivalent but is not managed by this manifest.)

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket whose canned ACL to set.

params.name: acl
params.label: Canned ACL
params.help: Canned ACL to apply. private is the safe default; public-read and public-read-write grant internet-facing access and require deliberate engineer review.

operations.title: Change bucket namespace scope
operations.description: Sets the `bucket_namespace` argument (account-regional or global) that determines the bucket's naming/addressing scope. Changing it forces replacement: Terraform destroys and recreates the bucket, and all stored objects are lost unless separately preserved. Engineer-authored, reviewed, and irreversible in place.

params.name: bucket
params.label: S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket whose namespace scope to change (will be replaced).

params.name: bucket_namespace
params.label: Bucket namespace
params.help: global (default) or account-regional. Changing this destroys and recreates the bucket.

operations.title: Set public access block skip_destroy flag
operations.description: Sets the skip_destroy flag on the bucket's public-access-block resource. When true, a future terraform destroy retains the public-access-block in AWS and merely removes it from Terraform state (the four blocking flags stay enforced on the bucket); when false (the default), destroy removes the public-access-block from AWS. Takes effect only on a future destroy, not on the live bucket now.

params.name: pab
params.label: Public access block config
params.help: The bucket's public-access-block resource to change.

params.name: skip_destroy
params.label: Skip destroy (retain on destroy)
params.help: When true, terraform destroy leaves the public-access-block in AWS and only removes it from state; false restores default destroy behavior.

operations.title: Set incomplete multipart upload abort days
operations.description: Sets rule.abort_incomplete_multipart_upload.days_after_initiation on an existing lifecycle rule — the number of days after a multipart upload is initiated before S3 aborts it and deletes the uploaded parts. Only affects orphaned incomplete uploads, never committed objects.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule to modify.

params.name: days_after_initiation
params.label: Abort after (days)
params.help: Days after a multipart upload is initiated before S3 aborts it and deletes its parts; positive integer (minimum 1).

operations.title: Toggle expired object delete marker cleanup
operations.description: Sets rule.expiration.expired_object_delete_marker on an existing lifecycle rule. When true, S3 removes an object's delete marker once it has no remaining noncurrent versions; no retrievable data is affected. Conflicts with a date/days expiration on the same block.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule to modify.

params.name: expired_object_delete_marker
params.label: Remove expired object delete markers
params.help: When true, S3 removes an object's delete marker once it has no noncurrent versions; when false, no action is taken.

operations.title: Set fixed-date storage class transition
operations.description: Sets rule.transition.date on an existing lifecycle rule — a fixed RFC3339 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which matching objects transition to the rule's target storage class. Affects cost/retrieval speed only, not deletion; conflicts with a day-based transition (rule.transition.days) on the same block.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule whose transition to modify.

params.name: transition_date
params.label: Transition on date (YYYY-MM-DD)
params.help: RFC3339 full-date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which objects transition storage class, e.g. 2027-01-01.

operations.title: Set noncurrent version storage class transition
operations.description: Configures rule.noncurrent_version_transition on an existing lifecycle rule: after an object version has been noncurrent for the given number of days it transitions to the chosen storage class, optionally keeping the most recent N noncurrent versions in their current class. Transition only — no deletion.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule to modify.

params.name: noncurrent_days
params.label: Transition after (noncurrent days)
params.help: Days a version stays noncurrent before transitioning; positive integer (minimum 1).

params.name: storage_class
params.label: Target storage class
params.help: Target storage class for noncurrent versions.

params.name: newer_noncurrent_versions
params.label: Keep newer noncurrent versions
params.help: Optional: keep this many of the most recent noncurrent versions in the current class before transitioning older ones; non-zero positive integer (minimum 1).

operations.title: Set noncurrent version expiration
operations.description: Configures rule.noncurrent_version_expiration on an existing lifecycle rule: permanently deletes object versions after they have been noncurrent for the given number of days, optionally retaining the most recent N noncurrent versions. This permanently removes the recovery copies versioning exists to protect — irreversible data loss — so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule to modify.

params.name: noncurrent_days
params.label: Expire after (noncurrent days)
params.help: Days a version stays noncurrent before it is permanently deleted; positive integer (minimum 1). Deletion is irreversible.

params.name: newer_noncurrent_versions
params.label: Keep newer noncurrent versions
params.help: Optional: retain this many of the most recent noncurrent versions; older ones are permanently deleted; non-zero positive integer (minimum 1).

operations.title: Set fixed-date object expiration
operations.description: Sets rule.expiration.date on an existing lifecycle rule — a fixed RFC3339 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which matching current object versions are permanently deleted. Irreversible data loss if misconfigured, so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: rule_id
params.label: Rule ID
params.help: The ID of the lifecycle rule whose expiration to modify.

params.name: expiration_date
params.label: Expire on date (YYYY-MM-DD)
params.help: RFC3339 full-date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which current object versions are permanently deleted, e.g. 2027-01-01. Deletion is irreversible.

operations.title: Set expected bucket owner account ID
operations.description: Sets expected_bucket_owner (the 12-digit AWS account ID of the expected bucket owner) on the lifecycle configuration resource as a safety guard against operating on a bucket that has changed hands. This argument is deprecated and forces replacement of the lifecycle configuration resource (its rules are briefly removed and re-created; the bucket and its data stay available throughout).

params.name: lifecycle_config
params.label: Bucket lifecycle configuration
params.help: The bucket's lifecycle configuration to modify.

params.name: expected_bucket_owner
params.label: Expected bucket owner account ID
params.help: 12-digit AWS account ID expected to own the bucket; the operation fails if the live bucket owner differs. Deprecated and forces replacement of the lifecycle configuration resource.
